emails.subject: EYP 2025 – Monday Recap + Tuesday Schedule
emails.body:
Dear Karthik,

Thank you for joining us today for the first day of *Engineering Your Ph.D. (EYP) 2025*! We hope the sessions gave you a strong start to your doctoral journey and introduced you to the people and resources that will support you along the way.

As promised, attached are PDF versions of today's presentations for your reference.

*Tomorrow's Schedule – Tuesday, August 26*

*Location: Carleton Commons (Mudd, 4th floor)*

   - 11:00 – 11:15 AM – Welcome and Brief Overview
   - 11:15 – 12:15 PM – CTL: Essentials of Teaching
   - 12:15 – 1:00 PM – Lunch
   - 1:00 – 2:00 PM – SEAS TA Panel
   - 2:00 – 3:00 PM – Inclusion and Belonging

*Looking Ahead to Thursday*

   -

   We will close EYP with a *Toast Reception* on *Thursday, August 28, from 1:00 – 3:00 PM in Carleton Commons, *a chance to gather together and celebrate the start of your Ph.D. journey.
   -

   In addition, we are planning a *Thursday evening social event*. More details will follow soon. Stay tuned!

We look forward to seeing you back tomorrow as we continue with sessions on teaching, the TA experience, and building a sense of belonging within the Columbia Engineering community.

Best regards,
*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering
emails.snippet: Thank you for joining us today for the first day of Engineering Your Ph.D. (EYP) 2025!

emails.subject: Welcome New CS PhD Students
emails.body:
Dear New PhD Students,

I am the Director of IT for the Department of Computer Science. The CS IT team is Computing Research Facilities (CRF) and we will be working with you during your time here.

We can be reached at crf@cs.columbia.edu.

Please read over the following items, especially the first one regarding CS account creation.

1. Your Computer Science Account

We need to create your CS account. Please note that if you were here previously and already have an account with the same name as your UNI, you should still apply for a new one and we will transfer your home directory over.

Your account name can be whatever you like as long as it is available.

I'd like to encourage you to choose an account name that is both readable and professional, keeping in mind that it will also be used for your CS email (see item 2). You can use your name (such as nancysmith or nsmith) as long as it is not already in use.

Account request page: https://www.cs.columbia.edu/crf/account-form/

emails.subject: Food Bank For New York City Opportunity Sign-Up Confirmation
emails.body:
Dear Karthik Sreedhar,

Thank you for registering to participate in Mobile Pantry @Chinatown YMCA (MANHATTAN) from 9/12/2025, 10:00 AM until 9/12/2025, 2:00 PM. We are excited to have you volunteer with us!

LOCATION: Chinatown Branch YMCA
100 Hester Street New York NY, United States 10002

WHAT TO WEAR:
Volunteers are expected to present a neat appearance and dress according to the requirements of the volunteer shift.

We ask that volunteers do not wear revealing attire such as muscle shirts, crop tops, "short shorts" or see-through clothing. Any shorts or skirts worn must be below the knee.

Sturdy, closed-toe, closed-heel shoes, such as athletic sneakers or boots, are required for safety. Ballet flats, loafers, slides, sandals, flip flops, crocs, and high-heels are not allowed.

PLEASE NOTE:
All volunteers must agree to Food Bank for New York City's volunteer waivers in order to participate.

Volunteers are expected to arrive on-time and stay for the duration of the project. If for some reason you are no longer able to attend, please let us know as soon as possible, so another volunteer can take your place.

emails.subject: Welcome to Fall 2025 from EGSC - DR Applications Open Now!
emails.body:
Dear Graduate SEAS,

Welcome all new and returning students to the Fall 2025 semester!

For those who may not know us yet, the Engineering Graduate Student Council
(EGSC) is a student-run organization dedicated to:

   -

   Serving as your voice for student concerns
   -

   Driving positive change within our community
   -

   Hosting social events to help you unwind and connect
   -

   Fostering a sense of community among our graduate students, both within
   Columbia and across NYC

I’m excited to introduce you to the Executive Board
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/content/executive-board-2025  >!

[image: image.png]

Make sure you connect with us on Slack
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__cuegsc.slack.com_ssb_redirect&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=nRxLel2EjC6HI93OpMjdH2VPWEyK1PV7VIuDH66Em5o&e= >, Instagram
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.instagram.com_columbiaegsc&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=gNAornxXObj3zrfk0YYR2A-2f_wvFoVsUCKEwAzO8Bs&e= >, and look out for our weekly
newsletter every Tuesday from our Communications Chair, Jonovan (you should
have gotten one already yesterday)!

We're thrilled to represent you and have an action-packed semester ahead!
Get ready for:

   -

   Social events (on-campus, off-campus, and with schools across Columbia
   and NYC) 🎊
   -

   Alumni chats and panels 💬
   -

   Professional Development Scholarships 🎓
   -

   A boat cruise ⛴️
   -

   And much more!

Want to join us on EGSC? Become a Department Representative (DR)! APPLY NOW
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_jv6y8DXcdoqLpVXcA&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=A8GPbggf1SMxonaDJ0bK7GuN3waqPwkxWpQuZmVO7e8&e= >!

As a DR, you'll:

   -

   Be the liaison between your department and EGSC
   -

   Bring student ideas and concerns to the table
   -

   Help shape EGSC events by joining one of our committees
   <https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/content/committees  >

How to apply:

   -

   Applications are open until 11:59 PM, Friday, September 12th
   -

   Elections will be held the following week (September 15th & 16th)
   -

   Grad students in each department/program will vote on their department’s
   candidates
   -

   More details in the application form: APPLY HERE
   <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_jv6y8DXcdoqLpVXcA&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=WLP3oLJBrCHGS29Bv1pi6z-78Bu3v3MmTZb7sC-Q8bDQShOVqn5AB024plTqT28L&s=A8GPbggf1SMxonaDJ0bK7GuN3waqPwkxWpQuZmVO7e8&e= >

[image: image.png]

Can't commit to being a DR? No problem! You're still welcome to join our
Friday meetings (4:00 - 5:00 PM, starting September 19th) and share your
thoughts!

If you have any questions, concerns, or ideas for SEAS, please don't
hesitate to reach out to:

   -

   President Thomas Bina (president@egsc.seas.columbia.edu)
   -

   Vice President Jenny Gao (vp@egsc.seas.columbia.edu)
   -

   Or any other E-Board member

Best wishes,
-- 

*Thomas Bina*
President
Engineering Graduate Student Council <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >
*Columbia University *
president@egsc.seas.columbia.edu

emails.subject: Appy to be an Ambassador!
emails.body:
Columbia | Engineering.

Hello Karthik,                                              The Office of Graduate Admissions at Columbia Engineering is searching for volunteer Graduate Student Ambassadors!                       

                        We are looking for outgoing and enthusiastic current students who are excited to talk to prospective and admitted students about their Columba experience.                       

                        We aim to have ambassadors from all academic departments to represent our wide range of program offerings.                      

Hours: Expected 1-2 events a semester. While most events are to be fulfilled in person during the 9 am-5 pm business day, there will be opportunities for virtual evening and weekend hours.

                        Tasks include:                      

	                             Welcoming visitors to the Graduate Admissions Office & On-Campus events                          

	                             Leading campus tours (Mostly in-person but may be offered virtually)                          

	                             Attending and speaking at prospective and admitted student events (mainly as a panelist)                          

	                             Attending required trainings to become familiar with the programs offered at Columbia Engineering                          

	                             Work with the Admissions team to generate external content for blogs, emails, and social media posts                          

                        Benefits:                      

	                             Network! Meet a variety of people, including fellow students, prospective students, staff, faculty, and administration                          

	                             Experience! This role offers a transferable and resume-building experience                          

                        To Apply:                      

	                             Complete Online Application Form <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_xX3pSymmubmAcyTu5&d=DwIFAg&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=FsxirmszmvXtNjiwnN9WlYTU2gMTZGPDztgPy1iX2MifMugPB3k_ea-Qzx-bXw8R&s=fYQs38clx2EW0pCoFLgLNedgcMMLNONuWX-Ur6saiWY&e= >

                        Deadline:                      

	 Wednesday, September 17th, 2025

                        We will review applications and follow up with selected candidates for a brief interview. If you have any questions or would like more information about the position, please feel free to reach out to Ms. Natasha Stanislas at nzs2103@columbia.edu.                      

                        Thank you for considering this unique opportunity to contribute to our vibrant community. We look forward to welcoming you as a valued Student Ambassador at Columbia Engineering Graduate Admissions.                      

                      Best,

                      Gabrielle Gannon
                      Associate Dean of Graduate Admissions
                      Office of Graduate Admissions
                      The Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science
                      Columbia University
Office of Graduate Admissions
                      500 W. 120th Street
                      New York, NY 10027

                      Admissions
                      (212) 854-4688
